reasoning:
The question asks whether Alina Kashlinskaya will have a FIDE rank of #15 or better (as high or higher) on several future dates. She is currently ranked #15 on the April 2026 list with a rating of 2480. 
For 2026-04-19, the FIDE rankings on Wikipedia will almost certainly not have updated since lists are published monthly on the 1st. Thus, she will still be #15.
For the subsequent 2026 dates (May, July, October), she is in a highly competitive bracket (the players ranked #13 to #17 are within 15 points of each other). She has roughly a coin-flip chance of remaining in the top 15 each month, perhaps dipping slightly below 0.5 over time as younger rising stars (like Carissa Yip, Divya Deshmukh) continue to improve.
For the long term (2027 to 2036), Kashlinskaya will age from 32 to almost 43. Given the historical trajectory of female chess players and the influx of new talent, it is highly likely her ranking will gradually fall outside the top 15. The probabilities are kept moderately conservative to account for potential resurgences, but reflect a clear downward trend in the likelihood of her maintaining a peak ranking.
